content.question: Does early selection in 6-/8-year tracks causally drive Hungary's exceptionally high school segregation outcomes, or does it merely channel pre-existing demand for elite education that would produce segregation through alternative mechanisms (school choice, residential sorting, streaming) regardless of track structure?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a natural experiment or synthetic-control analysis comparing segregation outcomes in Hungarian municipalities that retained 6-/8-year tracks versus those that eliminated them, controlling for baseline socio-economic sorting and residential composition; interview school leaders and parents to map the causal chain linking track structure to enrollment decisions.
